verses.sanskrit: उपास्य रात्रि शेषम् तु शोणा कूले महर्षिभिः ।निशायाम् सुप्रभातायाम् विश्वामित्रो अभ्यभाषत ॥१-३५-१॥
verses.text:
On sojourning the remaining night on the bank of river Sona along with 
			great-sages, Viswamitra spoke when that night is elapsing into a sunny 
			daybreak. [1-35-1]

verses.sanskrit: सुप्रभाता निशा राम पूर्वा संध्या प्रवर्तते ।उत्तिष्ठ उत्तिष्ठ भद्रम् ते गमनाय अभिरोचय ॥१-३५-२॥
verses.text:
"Oh, Rama, night fared into a sunny morning, eastern day-spring is set 
			in, hence awake and arise, you be safe, ready yourself for further travel." 
			[1-35-2]

verses.sanskrit: तत् श्रुत्वा वचनम् तस्य कृत्वा पौर्व आह्णिक क्रियः ।गमनम् रोचयामास वाक्यम् च इदम् उवाच ह ॥१-३५-३॥
verses.text:
On hearing the words of Sage Vishvamitra, Rama woke up and on completion 
			of morning time religious activities he readied himself for further 
			journey and indeed spoke this sentence to the sage. [1-35-3]

verses.sanskrit: अयम् शोणः शुभ जलो गाधः पुलिन मण्डितः ।कतरेण पथा ब्रह्मन् संतरिष्यामहे वयम् ॥१-३५-४॥
verses.text:
"This River Sona is with auspicious waters and even adorned with dunes, 
			where it is so deep. Oh, Brahman, in which of the two ways, namely 
			by fordless waters or by fordable water with dunes, we have to cross 
			this river?" [1-35-4]

verses.sanskrit: एवम् उक्तः तु रामेण विश्वामित्रो अब्रवीत् इदम् ।एष पंथा मया उद्दिष्टो येन यान्ति महर्षयः ॥१-३५-५॥
verses.text:
When Rama spoke to him thus sage Vishvamitra replied, "I propose the 
			same route by which these great sages are going. [1-35-5]

verses.sanskrit: एवम् उक्त्वा महर्षयो विश्वमित्रेण धीमता ।पश्यन्तः ते प्रयाता वै वनानि विविधानि च ॥१-३५-६॥
verses.text:
Thus spoken by that astute Sage Vishvamitra, the other great sages travelled 
			on, indeed beholding diverse forests and their environs. [1-35-6]

verses.sanskrit: ते गत्वा दूरम् अध्वानम् गते अर्ध दिवसे तदा ।जाह्नवीम् सरिताम् श्रेष्ठाम् ददृशुर् मुनि सेविताम् ॥१-३५-७॥
verses.text:
On going a distance on their way, and after a lapse of half a day, then 
			they perceived the prominent one among rivers and the one adored by 
			sages, namely River Ganga. [1-35-7]

verses.sanskrit: ताम् दृष्ट्वा पुण्य सलिलाम् हंस सारस सेविताम् ।बभूवुर् मुनयः सर्वे मुदिता सह राघवाः ॥१-३५-८॥
verses.text:
On seeing River Ganga with her pious waters and adored by swans and 
			saarasa waterfowls, all the sages felt delighted together with both 
			of the Raghava-s, namely Rama and Lakshmana. [1-35-8]

verses.sanskrit: तस्याः तीरे ततः चक्रुः ते आवास परिग्रहम् ।ततः स्नात्वा यथा न्यायम् संतर्प्य पितृ देवताः ॥१-३५-९॥
verses.text:
Then they sojourned on the riverbank of Ganga, and next on taking bath 
			in Ganga they have customarily offered that river's water as oblations 
			to their manes. After that, on enkindling the ritual-fire they have 
			also offered fire-oblations into it for gods. Later, dining on their 
			part of those ambrosian oblations with which they have oblated the fire, 
			those sages perched on the propitious banks of Ganga, surrounding the 
			great-souled Vishvamitra from all sides, to the contentment of their 
			heart in having performed religious chores on the riverbank of Ganga. 
			[1-35-9, 10, 11a]

verses.sanskrit: हुत्वा चैव अग्निहोत्राणि प्राश्य च अमृतवत् हविः ।विविशुर् जाह्नवी तीरे शुभा मुदित मानसाः ॥१-३५-१०॥
विश्वामित्रम् महात्मानम् परिवार्य समंततः ।विष्टिताः च यथा न्यायम् राघवो च यथा अर्हम् ।संप्रहृष्ट मना रामो विश्वामित्रम् अथ अब्रवीत् ॥१-३५-११॥
verses.text:
When sages are sitting at ease then even Rama and Lakshmana sat down 
			according to their admissibility and custom of sitting before their 
			teachers, and then Rama highly gladdened at heart has spoken to Vishvamitra. 
			[1-35-11]

verses.sanskrit: भगवन् श्रोतुम् इच्छामि गङ्गाम् त्रि पथ गाम् नदीम् ।त्रैलोक्यम् कथम् आक्रम्य गता नद नदीपतिम् ॥१-३५-१२॥
verses.text:
"Oh, godly sage, I wish to listen about Ganga, the river that has a 
			trinal course, and as to how she is reaching the husband of rivulets 
			and rivers, namely the ocean, on pervading all the three worlds." Thus 
			Rama asked Vishvamitra. [1-35-12]

verses.sanskrit: चोदितो राम वाक्येन विश्वामित्रो महामुनिः ।वृद्धिम् जन्म च गंगाया वक्तुम् एव उपचक्रमे ॥१-३५-१३॥
verses.text:
Motivated by Rama's words the eminent-sage Vishvamitra commenced to 
			narrate about the emergence and that way even about the progression 
			of River Ganga. [1-35-13]

verses.sanskrit: शैलेन्द्रो हिमवान् राम धातूनाम् आकरो महान् ।तस्य कन्या द्वयम् राम रूपेण अप्रतिमम् भुवि ॥१-३५-१४॥
verses.text:
"The lordly mountain Himavan who is the greatest treasure trove of minerals, 
			Rama, also treasures up a pair of daughters who by their comeliness 
			are unequalled on earth. [1-35-14]

verses.sanskrit: या मेरु दुहिता राम तयोर् माता सुमध्यमा ।नाम्ना मेना मनोज्ञा वै पत्नी हिमवतः प्रिया ॥१-३५-१५॥
verses.text:
"The slender-waisted and fascinating daughter of Mt. Meru, oh, Rama, 
			renowned by her name Mena, is the dear wife of Himavan and the mother 
			of those two daughters, indeed. [1-35-15]

verses.sanskrit: तस्याम् गंग इयम् अभवत् ज्येष्ठा हिमवतः सुता ।उमा नाम द्वितीया अभूत् कन्या तस्य एव राघव ॥१-३५-१६॥
verses.text:
"This Ganga has emerged as an elder daughter to Himavan through Mena, 
			oh, Raghava, and that way a girl renowned by the name Uma has become 
			a second daughter to him. [1-35-16]

verses.sanskrit: अथ ज्येष्ठाम् सुराः सर्वे देव कार्य चिकीर्षया ।शैलेन्द्रम् वरयामासुः गंगाम् त्रि पथ गाम् नदीम् ॥१-३५-१७॥
verses.text:
"Later, all of the divinities intending to fulfil a divine purpose have 
			sought the lordly mountain Himavan to spare Ganga, who is scheduled 
			to become a tri-way-cruising river. [1-35-17]

verses.sanskrit: ददौ धर्मेण हिमवान् तनयाम् लोक पावनीम् ।स्वच्छंद पथ गाम् गंगाम् त्रैलोक्य हित काम्यया ॥१-३५-१८॥
verses.text:
"With a righteous thinking and hopeful of the welfare in triadic world, 
			Himavan then spared his daughter Ganga whose flow is at her own volition. 
			[1-35-18]

verses.sanskrit: प्रतिगृह्य त्रिलोक अर्थम् त्रिलोक हित कांक्षिणः ।गंगाम् आदाय ते अगच्छन् कृतार्थेन अंतरात्मना ॥१-३५-१९॥
verses.text:
"Those divinities who are the well-wishers of the well-being of tri-world 
			welcomed Ganga, who is incidentally intended for the purpose of the 
			tri-world, and they came back to heaven fetching Ganga with them, with 
			a heartfelt joy for their purpose is fulfilled. [1-35-19]

verses.sanskrit: या च अन्या शैल दुहिता कन्या आसीत् रघुनंदन ।उग्रम् सुव्रतम् आस्थाय तपः तेपे तपोधना ॥१-३५-२०॥
verses.text:
"The other daughter of Himavanta, oh, Raghu's descendent, that unmarried 
			girl namely Uma, she performed a rigorous sacrament taking hold of a 
			supreme devoutness as her ascesis itself is her wealth. [1-35-20]

verses.sanskrit: उग्रेण तपसा युक्ताम् ददौ शैलवरः सुताम् ।रुद्राय अप्रतिरूपाय उमाम् लोक नमस्कृताम् ॥१-३५-२१॥
verses.text:
"As for Uma who is unified with her rigorous sacrament and who is adored 
			by all the world, that best one among mountains Himavanta gave such 
			a daughter to the unique god Rudra. [1-35-21]

verses.sanskrit: एते ते शैल राजस्य सुते लोक नमस्कृते ।गंगा च सरिताम् श्रेष्ठा उमादेवी च राघव ॥१-३५-२२॥
verses.text:
"Oh, Raghava, the prominent one among rivers namely Ganga and Uma Devi 
			as well, these are the two daughters of the king of mountains that are 
			highly revered by the world. [1-35-22]

verses.sanskrit: एतत् ते सर्वम् आख्यातम् यथा त्रि पथ गामिनी ।खम् गता प्रथमम् तात गतिम् गतिमताम् वर ॥१-३५-२३॥
verses.text:
"Dear boy Rama with beautiful gait, as to how the triply coursing Ganga 
			has cruised to heaven in the first phase, I have related all about that 
			cruise to you. [1-35-23]

verses.sanskrit: स एषा सुर नदी रम्या शैलेन्द्र तनया तदा ।सुर लोकम् समारूढा विपापा जल वाहिनी ॥१-३५-२४॥
verses.text:
"As such, that marvellous and sinless daughter of the lordly mountain 
			Himavanta, ever-flowing in the form of water, ascended to the abode 
			of divinities and became devaganga , Divine 
			River. [1-35-24]
